item.title: Counsel
item.description: Build a career with impact. Working at the World Bank Group (WBG) provides a unique opportunity to help countries solve their greatest development challenges. As one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries, the WBG is a unique partnership of five global institutions dedicated to ending poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, the WBG works with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. Do you want to build a career that is truly worthwhile? The Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (MIGA) is a member of the World Bank Group, and our mission is to promote foreign direct investment into developing countries to help end extreme poverty and boost share prosperity. We do that by providing political risk insurance and credit enhancement to investors and lenders against losses caused by non-commercial risks. Since July 1, 2024, all World Bank Group guarantee products are being processed by MIGA in the new World Bank Group (WBG) Guarantee Platform, which brings together guarantee products and experts from across the World Bank, the International Finance Corporation (IFC), and the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (MIGA). This integration enhances efficiency, simplicity, and speed in delivering guarantee solutions. For more information, visit www.miga.org. The Legal Affairs and Claims Group in MIGA (known as “MIGLC”) serves as the legal counsel for MIGA and the WBG Guarantee Platform and provides legal advice to IBRD/IDA, IFC and MIGA in relation to all guarantee operations. Among its principal responsibilities, MIGLC advises the Board of Directors and senior management of the relevant WBG institution on legal aspect of its guarantee products, drafts and negotiates the contract of guarantee and other relevant legal documentation, facilitates discussions between investors and governments related to disputes and potential claims, and oversees outside counsel. MIGA also promotes innovation and knowledge sharing in support of the WBG’s mission and the WBG Guarantee Platform. MIGLC also advises MIGA’s Council of Governors, Board of Directors and senior management on all legal issues in connection with MIGA’s governance and operations. MIGLC supports MIGA’s internal departments in risk and policy analysis, finance and risk management and external outreach. For MIGA guarantees, MIGLC also manages all claims proceedings. Roles Responsibility: The Counsel will be expected to work on a diversified portfolio of legal issues, across all sectors and geographic areas. The Counsel’s duties and responsibilities are listed below: • Provide legal advice on guarantee operations of the WBG Guarantee Platform, including transaction structures and eligibility under MIGA’s and IBRD’s/IDA’s governing documents and applicable policies and procedures. • As part of a project team and under the guidance of a Chief Counsel, advise on project risks, conduct due diligence to assess risks that affect the rights of the relevant WBG entity as insurer and subrogee, contribute to approval documents, draft and negotiate contracts of guarantee and related legal documents. • Oversee work of outside counsel, when retained, and manage costs. • Assist MIGA’s portfolio team in the preparation of waivers, consents, amendments, approval documents, non-disclosure agreements, and other legal documents relating to guarantees processed under the WBG Guarantee Platform. • Research and draft memoranda on issues of national and public international law and conducting legal due diligence related to host countries. • Assist in the process of obtaining and/or modifying required host country approval for guarantees. • Assist with review and management of disputes between investors and host countries that may develop into claims under WBG guarantees. • Carry out other duties assigned by the General Counsel or a Chief Counsel.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: ET Consultant - Senior Evaluation Officer
item.description: The World Bank Group is one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries; a unique global partnership of five institutions dedicated to ending extreme poverty and promoting shared prosperity. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, we work with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. For more information, visit www.worldbank.org. The Global Environment Facility (GEF) is a financial mechanism that provides grant and concessional funds to recipient countries for projects and activities to protect the global environment. The governance structure of the GEF includes an Assembly, a Council, a Secretariat, a Scientific and Technical Advisory Panel and an independent Evaluation Office. Projects financed by the GEF are managed by three Implementing Agencies (UNDP, UNEP and the World Bank) and seven Executing Agencies. The GEF Evaluation Office is functionally independent of, but administratively supported by, the World Bank. It is independent from the CEO of the GEF and reports directly to the GEF Council. The GEF Council decides on M E policies, discusses four-year rolling work plans, approves annual budgets, and receives all evaluation reports. The GEF Independent Evaluation Office has the central role of ensuring the independent evaluation function within the GEF; of setting minimum requirements for monitoring and evaluation; of ensuring oversight of the quality of M E systems on program and project level and of sharing evaluative evidence within the GEF. A consultative process with relevant GEF partners is in place to ensure the quality of the work undertaken and to prevent overlap and duplication. The Office has the responsibility for undertaking independent evaluations which involve a set of projects from more than one Implementing and/or Executing Agency. These evaluations are typically on a strategic level, or on focal areas, or on cross-cutting themes. The GEF IEO is based in Washington, DC. All positions in the Office are World Bank Group appointments. More information about the IEO can be found at Office’s website https://www.gefieo.org Duties and Accountabilities The Senior Evaluation Officer leads complex corporate, thematic, and programmatic evaluations of GEF strategies, policies, and interventions, contributing to institutional accountability, learning, and improvement. The position entails responsibility for evaluation design and methodology, oversight of data collection and analysis, management of multidisciplinary teams and budgets, and the drafting of major evaluation reports. The Senior Evaluation Officer engages with senior stakeholders within the GEF partnership, presents evaluation findings to the GEF Council, and contributes to shaping learning agendas to enhance the effectiveness, credibility, and utility of GEF evaluations. The senior officer will assist in planning and implementing M E policies and activities as follows: 1. Leadership of Evaluations • Lead, co-lead, or serve as senior member of complex thematic, programmatic, and corporate evaluations assessing the effectiveness, relevance, efficiency, and sustainability of GEF interventions. • Provide leadership across the full evaluation cycle, including conceptualization, preparation of approach papers, design of frameworks and methods, data collection, analysis, and drafting and finalization of reports. • Contribute to evaluations across key thematic areas—biodiversity, climate change, international waters, land degradation, chemicals and waste—as well as integrated and cross-cutting approaches, and other areas as required. • Present findings to senior management, the GEF Council, and other governance bodies, and support follow-up and learning processes. • Lead or contribute to synthesis notes, learning products, post-completion verifications, and other cross-cutting analytical outputs, in coordination with knowledge management and communications colleagues to ensure effective dissemination and uptake of findings. 2. Methodological Leadership and Quality Assurance • Provide methodological leadership and quality assurance, ensuring high standards in evaluation design, analytical rigor, evidence use, and reporting. • Promote the use of advanced analytical methods, as well as innovative tools suited to environmental evaluation. • Ensure consistent and appropriate application of methodologies under real-world constraints (time, data, and resources), while safeguarding independence and credibility. • Contribute to internal training and learning initiatives on evaluation methods and analytical tools, including structured modules and hands-on support. 3. Team Leadership, Capacity Development, and External Engagement • Lead, supervise, mentor, and coach junior staff and consultants, fostering a collaborative, inclusive, high-performing work environment, and providing technical advice to colleagues and management. • Represent the IEO in engagements with GEF Agencies, the Secretariat, partner institutions, and external stakeholders, and participate in international evaluation networks and professional forums. • Undertake mission travel as required to support evaluation activities and stakeholder engagement.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: E T Temporary
item.description: Do you want to build a career that is truly worthwhile? Working at the World Bank provides a unique opportunity for you to join us to help our clients solve their greatest development challenges. The World Bank (WB) is one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries; a unique global partnership of five institutions (IBRD, IDA, IFC, MIGA, and ICSID) dedicated to ending extreme poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development on a livable planet. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, we work with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. For more information, visit www.worldbank.org. The Controllers (CTR) Vice Presidency is responsible for all aspects of the financial reporting and internal control framework for IBRD and IDA, and the extensive Trust Fund/Partnership portfolio of the WB entities. It supports an appropriate fiduciary control framework and administering disbursements for Bank lending and leads the client service function for the WB’s corporate expenses. Organizationally, CTR is comprised of three departments: (1) Corporate Accounting and Reporting, (2) Trust Funds and Loans, and (3) Financial Controls. In CTR, our mandate supports both internal and external clients while we focus on delivery, optimization, impact, and people culture. CTR Financial Operations (CTRFO) is a business division in CTR and is responsible for the fair recording and reporting of the WBG’s administrative expenses and fixed assets, which have a combined financial statement value of nearly $4.0B. This includes delivering core accounting services, maintaining an effective system of internal controls, and providing strategic leadership on complex business issues pertaining to the following administrative expenses and functions: (i) Travel Accounting, Accounts Payable, Fixed Asset and Lease Accounting, Quality Assurance and Country Office Accounting (ii) Accounts Receivable, Payroll and Tax Allowance Administration and (iii) Helpdesk Operations Unit Overview: CTRHP is located at Chennai and responsible for processing vendor payments for all HQ units in Washington DC. CTRHP reports functionally to CTRTA. This team complements and works with CTRFC’s other functional teams in Chennai: Payroll, Tax and AR (CTRPA), the Assurance and Client Support team (CTRAC), which deliver core transaction processing services related to Accounts Receivable, Payroll, Tax, provide advisory and support services to country office accountants, travelers and WBG staff in accounting and compliance activities through training, reviews and help desk operations (CTRAN). CTRHP is looking to recruit a suitable candidate for the ETT position in the global payments team. Note: This is an ETT appointment. The selected candidate would have to follow hybrid work model based out of Chennai, India. The ETT will be responsible for processing payments to vendors and staff located at HQ or Country offices. The job requires extensive interaction with HQ (or) the country offices accounting staff and vendors. What will you do: • Process payments to staff and vendors within service standards and contractual terms, which include invoice review, data entry, and payment proposal preparation, review, and release in accordance with established guidelines. • Track and clear invoice work items assigned in the SAP inbox; check emails received from clients and take immediate action; resolve cases that require special attention. • Follow up with TTLs, managers, other staff, and vendors regarding pending workflow items. • Apply provisions of US GAAP for accrual accounting. • Adopt best practices to set off vendor receivables. • Research and initiate action for vendor debit balances; work with vendor on refunds. • Investigate and resolve cases for failed payment executions, stop payment requests, and stale-dated checks. • Execute vendor down-payments, as needed for prepaid expenses and advances and ensure these are netted against subsequent invoices; monitor and resolve open down-payments. • Execute daily Evaluated Receipts Settlement (ERS) process to generate invoice documents in SAP from electronically approved service entry sheets. • Provide resolution to vendor claims relating to non-payment or incomplete payment or insufficient information to apply payments. • Respond to queries from vendors and units relating to invoice payments. • Analyze / research / act on requests for accounting adjustments relating to accounts payable, engage in discussion as needed to understand and determine action required. • Submit employee reimbursement request for miscellaneous payments to consultants and non-PO payments to firms! • Coordinate with the vendor master team to update banking information! • Maintain excellent client rapport and demonstrate client service skills in resolving issues. • Participate in ad-hoc activities such as system testing and other assigned tasks. Note: The Working Hour will be 8.5 hours between 07.00 AM to 10.30 PM (IST). The candidate should be willing to work in any of the slots as per business requirement.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: Evaluation Analyst
item.description: Build a career with impact. Working at the World Bank Group (WBG) provides a unique opportunity to help countries solve their greatest development challenges. As one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries, the WBG is a unique partnership of five global institutions dedicated to ending poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, the WBG works with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. Do you want to build a career that is truly worthwhile? The Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (MIGA) is a member of the World Bank Group, and our mission is to promote foreign direct investment into developing countries to help end extreme poverty and boost shared prosperity. We do that through the provision of political risk insurance and credit enhancement to investors and lenders against losses caused by non‑commercial risks. For more information, visit www.miga.org. MIGA’s Economics and Sustainability Group (MIGES) consists of several areas of excellence: Economics, Environment and Social, Climate, Gender, and Evaluations. The Evaluation Unit (MIGEV) is seeking an Evaluation Analyst (GE) who is dynamic, proactive, meticulous, and a team player. The successful candidate will bring strong analytical skills and experience in monitoring and evaluating development outcomes in complex projects in both public and private sectors, with a solid financial and technical background. The Analyst will support MIGA’s self‑evaluation of projects, contribute to the operational effectiveness of MIGA evaluation practices, and assist in coordination with the Independent Evaluation Group (IEG). The Analyst will also support the collection and organization of results data and performance indicators, including those for the WBG Corporate Scorecard FY24–30 and communications on operational results and the distinctive roles of guarantee instruments—emphasizing green, resilient, and inclusive development by mobilizing private capital and working as One World Bank Group. (Aligned with the WBG Scorecard framework and MIGA DEIS guidance.) Duties and Responsibilities The Evaluation Analyst is expected to work with a moderate degree of autonomy under the overall supervision of the Manager in MIGES and guidance of the Chief Evaluation Officer. Contribute to MIGA’s self‑evaluation program • Support the self evaluation cycle for MIGA projects: compile evidence packages, clean and analyze data, and draft analytical sections in line with MIGA’s evaluative approach and data standards. • Apply agreed methodologies and incorporate lessons from prior exercises; maintain source traceability and document repositories. • Contribute to efficiency improvements in evaluation processes and tools; flag methodological gaps and propose options for Officer/Manager review. • Assist discussions on the review and update of IEG evaluation methodologies (background preparation, benchmarking, and note taking). Contribute to Monitoring and Results Reporting at MIGA • Support initiatives to improve efficiency of development data collection systems and processes (e.g., client data workflows and internal curation). (Aligned with DEIS guidance.) • Gather and consolidate inputs for WBG/MIGA Corporate Scorecard (CSC) reporting with appropriate quality checks and reproducibility. (Aligned with Scorecard FY24–30 framework.) • Assist engagement with WBG counterparts to ensure consistent application of Scorecard methodologies. • Support evaluations, audits, mid term reviews, and updates to the Scorecard approach as needed, with a focus on WBG guarantee projects. (Internal process notes on CSC.) • Coordinate with MIGES Economics on the IMPACT (Ex Ante Development Impact Framework) inputs and indicator tracing for guarantees. Contribute to MIGA’s review of IEG’s evaluation reports and products • Apply professional and evaluation standards to assist validation of IEG’s evaluative work on MIGA (portfolio scans, methods checks, evidence synthesis). • Prepare comparative analyses using appropriate quantitative and qualitative methods; support discussions with operational, legal, and management stakeholders on performance and evaluative judgments. • Coordinate draft comments and feedback to IEG with relevant WBG counterparts; prepare high quality drafts and summaries for Officer/Manager review. Act as counterpart for IEG’s evaluative activities for MIGA • Track IEG evaluation schedules and proactively facilitate requests for data, documents, and meetings. • Engage with IEG teams to organize underlying data and materials in anticipation of report reviews and recommendation follow up. • Identify lessons from project evaluations, monitoring data, and external evidence; prepare quarterly summaries of salient lessons for MIGA projects. • Draft knowledge assets (briefs, slide decks) and support dissemination, including internal events (e.g., BBLs) led by Officers/Management. • Help create and maintain improved record systems for evaluation reports and related information to be shared within MIGA and across the WBG.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: WBG Pioneer - Research Intern
item.description: WBG Pioneer Background/Organizational Context The World Bank Group’s Women, Business and the Law (WBL) project examines laws and regulations affecting women’s prospects as entrepreneurs and employees across 190 economies. Its goal is to inform policy discussions on how to remove legal restrictions on women and promote research on how to improve women’s economic inclusion. The Women, Business and the Law index serves as a road map for reform by capturing three interdependent pillars that together shape women’s access to jobs: laws, policies and institutions, and enforcement perceptions across ten topics: Safety, Mobility, Work, Pay, Marriage, Parenthood, Childcare, Entrepreneurship, Assets, and Pension. Duties and Responsibilities The fellow will primarily work on data collection and analysis for Women, Business and the Law. The position requires the ability to work in a fast-paced and multicultural environment, strong analytical and research skills as well as robust detail-orientation. Duties of the fellow will include, but not be limited to, the following: • Conduct country and/or topic specific research on legislation impacting women’s economic empowerment in the context of WBL indicators. • Research indicator-specific laws, regulations, and policies through primary data sources and publicly available information to support coding decisions. • Support data analysis and the preparation of written outputs such as briefs, blogs or presentations. • Assist with respondent outreach, including administering surveys to local experts, leading new local partners’ recruitment and maintaining existing networks, and collecting data on WBL indicators. • Deliver updated information for WBL data, including assessing survey responses with primary data sources and following up with survey respondents until a final, verified country profile is produced. • Work on knowledge and learning activities to help World Bank staff develop a more informed view on how legal gender equality relates to economic growth and poverty reduction. • Substantially contribute to several of the Women, Business and the Law functions: production, dissemination, contributor relationship, management, website, media, data analysis, etc. • Support the translation of project documents. • Provide general support to the project, as required.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: WBG Pioneer - Legal Research Intern
item.description: WBG Pioneer Background/Organizational Context The World Bank Group’s Women, Business and the Law (WBL) project examines laws and regulations affecting women’s prospects as entrepreneurs and employees across 190 economies. Its goal is to inform policy discussions on how to remove legal restrictions on women and promote research on how to improve women’s economic inclusion. The Women, Business and the Law index serves as a road map for reform by capturing three interdependent pillars that together shape women’s access to jobs: laws, policies and institutions, and enforcement perceptions across ten topics: Safety, Mobility, Work, Pay, Marriage, Parenthood, Childcare, Entrepreneurship, Assets, and Pension. Duties and Responsibilities Comparative legal research • Conduct country and/or topic specific research on legislation impacting women’s economic empowerment in the context of WBL topics. • Deliver updated information for WBL indicators, including assessing survey responses with primary data sources and following up with survey respondents until a final, verified country file is produced. • Conduct broader research on specific indicator topics addressing women’s rights; • Produce comparative data analyses related to the assigned set of indicators. • Support the systematic archiving, organization, and maintenance of legal information and documents in the Women, Business and the Law database. Applied economic research • Organize and manage project tasks for the implementation of the Enforcement Perceptions Pillar data collection, tracking, follow-up procedures, and perception data analysis. • Prepare presentations and briefing documents for management and stakeholders regarding the WBL data collection cycle and results. • Support annual data and report production, documentation and writing, data analysis, and communication among other WBL teams to ensure data quality and reproducibility. • Conduct econometric analysis of WBL data and draft research outputs using WBL data, support dissemination. Stakeholder management • Assist with contributor outreach, including administering surveys to local experts, leading new local partners’ recruitment, and collecting data on WBL topics. • Maintaining and expanding partner relationships, implementing strategies to strengthen contributor engagement and retention. • Updating contributor data and reporting systems, developing acknowledgment materials, conducting quality assurance reviews. • Supporting the identification and engagement of Government Focal Points. • Assisting with the expansion of the National Statistical Offices contributor base, to strengthen WBL’s government engagement and partner outreach. Dissemination activities • Preparing presentations, talking points, briefing materials, outreach documents, newsletters, training resources, etc. for workshops, webinars, stakeholder meetings, and dissemination activities for internal and external audiences. • Developing and implementing social media and communications strategies around key events, including written content and graphics. • Measuring reach of dissemination activities via media monitoring and reporting to support WBL’s communications goals and raise awareness of its work and research. • Supporting the planning, organization, and coordination of WBL hybrid events and dissemination activities, including the annual report launch. • Provide general support to the project, as required.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: Manager - Procurement Operations & Advisory (WBPPS)
item.description: Note: “This vacancy announcement is for one position posted across multiple possible duty stations. That includes Washington D.C and Sofia and Chennai. The selected candidate's duty station will be determined based on organizational requirements and business needs. If the selection is based in Washington, DC, the recruitment type will be International and if the selection is based in Sofia or Chennai, the recruitment type will be Local.” The World Bank Group is a unique global partnership of five institutions driven by a bold vision to create a world free of poverty on a livable planet. As one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries, we help solve the world’s greatest development challenges. When you join the World Bank Group, you become part of a dynamic, diverse organization with 189 member countries and 182 offices worldwide. We work with public and private sector partners, invest in groundbreaking projects, and use data, research, and technology to bring tangible and transformative change around the globe. For more information, visit www.worldbank.org Vice-Presidency Context The WBG Budget and Procurement (WBP) is organized into five Departments: WBP Centers of Excellence (WBPCE) with teams supporting the corporate strategic business and budget planning process, providing WBP systems, knowledge, and shared services; WBP IBRD/IDA Regional Units (WBPRU) with teams supporting IBRD/IDA clients in the regions; WBP IFC/MIGA Budget Partners (CSRDR) with teams supporting IFC/MIGA clients in the HQ and the regions; WBP Corporate Units (WBPCU) with teams supporting the Knowledge Bank and corporate units and providing capital budget support; and WBP Corporate Procurement (WBPCP) with teams providing group-wide procurement services. Unit Context Steps to continue evolving the management structure of WBPCP with its strategic priorities present the opportunity to recruit for an expanded managerial role in the Department. In line with the WBP global footprint strategy and delivery model, the location of the positions may be Washington D.C., Chennai, or Sofia. In accordance with World Bank Group policy, positions in Country Offices, irrespective of level, are subject to local recruitment. Selections to positions outside of Washington D.C. will be LRS. Business needs and candidate preference will be considered in determining the actual location of each of the positions described below: Manager, Procurement Operations Advisory is responsible for all operational and advisory functions within the Corporate Procurement department. These include the Corporate Procurement Service Desk, Order Management, Vendor Management, governance monitoring, systems support and administration, and regional procurement advisory, amongst others. Manages a global team of 35 staff based in the HQ, Global Business Centers (GBC), and in each of the seven regions of the WBG to support the decentralized footprint of WBG clients. Duties and accountabilities: Manager, WBPPS has the following three areas of responsibility: A)Business Management Responsibilities •Manage the teams providing planning, budgeting, and performance reporting services; conducting analytical work and providing advisory services in areas of focus (e.g., operations, investments in technology); and providing other services as needed over time. •Is an active member of the WBP Leadership Team, participates in cross cutting activities, and from time to time, takes on leadership roles in WBP-wide activities, such as recruitment and other HR initiatives. •Provides support and advice to the Vice President and the Director of WBP, Corporate Procurement as requested. B)Talent Management Responsibilities •Manages the unit staff, including recruitment, evaluation, and career development of staff. •Provides leadership and support to her/his staff to build sustainable finance skills and capacity to effectively deliver WBP services. •Manages and oversees the engagement and evaluation of technical consultants within the WBPCP unit, as needed. C)Budget Management Responsibilities •Effectively and efficiently manages his/her unit’s resources, ensuring its effective formulation and input into budget decisions. •Advocates and drives cost-effective delivery of business finance services, promoting transparency, simplicity, standardization, and automation.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: Regional Industry Manager, Infrastructure, Europe
item.description: IFC — a member of the World Bank Group — is the largest global development institution focused on the private sector in emerging markets. We work in more than 100 countries, using our capital, expertise, and influence to create markets and opportunities in developing countries. IFC leverages private sector solutions and mobilizes private capital to create more, better and inclusive jobs in order to end extreme poverty and boost shared prosperity on a livable planet. For more information, visit www.ifc.org. IFC strategy also aims to stimulate more investment activity across emerging markets by proactively working to create, deepen, and expand markets and to design and develop potential investment projects. Achieving these growth and impact ambitions at scale requires a different approach to business development, deal sourcing, and client support that relies on i) a broader product offering to engage earlier in the project cycle and secure opportunities; ii) a greater focus on preparing clients in nascent markets and sectors for investment; iii) a greater focus on value addition and risk management of portfolio investment clients to enhance their performance; and iv) a longer time horizon and a more patient, longer-term perspective. To drive this approach, IFC is seeking a Regional Industry Manager for its infrastructure, energy, mining, municipal, water and digital (INR) activities in the Europe region covering Poland, Ukraine, Moldova, Georgia, Armenia and Azerbaijan. The Manager will lead and coordinate IFC's efforts to enable investment opportunities, enhance investment outcomes, improve investment conditions, and deliver INR investment activities. The role combines upstream market creation and project development with business development, transaction processing, portfolio management, and client engagement. The Manager will be based in Paris, France and will be expected to frequently visit all countries and other offices where the Europe infrastructure team and clients are based. The Manager will report to IFC’s Regional Industry Director for INR for Europe, and will be a member of the Europe INR management team and the IFC Europe Regional Leadership Team, and will be expected to work in partnership with IFC’s Division Directors, WBG Country Managers, IFC Program Managers, other Regional Industry Managers/Directors, WBG Global Knowledge Bank counterparts, IBRD counterparts, and also to coordinate closely across IFC/WBG departments and divisions, including Treasury, Legal, Risk, Special Operations, IFC Products and Clients, Environmental Social, Partnerships, and other relevant teams. The Manager will have direct supervisory responsibility for a team of approximately 20 staff while also collectively managing the IFC Europe INR team of approximately 40 people jointly with the INR Regional Industry Manager based in Vienna ensure the broader Europe INR team operates as a single high-performing, well-motivated team to develop and deliver the full spectrum of IFC’s INR activities across the entire Europe region. Duties and Accountabilities The roles and responsibilities of the position will include but not be limited to: Strategic Responsibility • Develop the strategy for IFC’s INR activities in the region, setting key business goals focused on high-impact projects aligned with IFC country strategies and diagnostics. • Link policy reforms, regulatory interventions, upstream market creation, investment, and mobilization to deliver country-level solutions and unlock private investment opportunities, including by leveraging the WBG's knowledge and lending programs. • Engage with and establish partnerships with thought leaders, decision makers, clients, sponsors, WBG counterparts, and other external parties to foster innovative thinking, create new markets, identify collaboration opportunities, and enhance business delivery. • Proactively convene stakeholders and connect expertise from upstream teams, investment staff, industry specialists, country teams, clients, sponsors, and other partners to enable delivery of upstream and investment operations. • Participate in and contribute to regional leadership teams, global sector teams, and cross-sector and cross-departmental collaboration to advance IFC's strategic priorities. Business Responsibility • Lead INR business development efforts together with regional/country and global industry teams; manage selected client relationships; build and maintain high-level relationships with clients; and ensure high client satisfaction and program delivery within impact, profitability, and risk parameters. • Identify, incubate, and develop high-impact projects and initiatives that require upstream project development to reach the point of investment, including preparing potential clients for IFC investment and supporting sector-wide change in priority areas. • Lead multidisciplinary teams to turn leads into transactions that balance IFC's additionality, financial risks and rewards, sustainable development impact, innovation, replication, and scalability. • Execute investment operations by supporting business development, providing credit and investment guidance, ensuring quality control of teams' work, and assuming Tier I ADM responsibility for the processing quality investments. • Manage upstream operations with a focus on sound project design across the project cycle and portfolio, high development effectiveness, appropriate deployment of technical expertise, and exceptional client service. • Actively manage profitability for the INR business, revenue and net income generation, use of economic capital, and long-term financial sustainability, including through sales, risk sharing, and mobilization where appropriate. • Supervise and manage the debt and equity portfolio, including portfolio reviews, risk monitoring, early warning for assets at risk, loan-loss reserve recommendations, equity management plans, valuations, write-downs, exit strategies, and board seat proposals for strategic assets. • Coordinate with CSO, Risk, Treasury, Legal, Equity colleagues, Environmental Social, and other relevant teams on risk, restructuring, rescheduling, compliance, and portfolio matters. • Ensure portfolio experience and lessons learned are fed into strategy development and business decision making, including through benchmarking analytics and portfolio insights. • Manage funding resources effectively by optimizing internal and external funding and budgets for upstream operations and contributing to donor engagement strategy, relationship management, and reporting through collaboration with Partnerships. • Work with other IFC industry departments and regional managers to identify joint ventures and cross-sector opportunities to expand IFC's business. Knowledge Capture and Management • Spot game-changing trends, technologies, and business models that can increase development impact and present opportunities for high-value proprietary engagements. • Disseminate and absorb knowledge and experience across the regional industry department, WBG counterparts, and global teams to support adoption of new approaches and best practices. • Build external brand recognition and recognition of IFC's distinctive capabilities in working upstream, catalyzing investment opportunities, and delivering high-quality INR investments. • Produce and use portfolio analytics, benchmarking, and lessons learned to inform strategy, risk management, and business development. People, Team, and Administrative Management • Lead and manage the unit and relevant staff to ensure high performance, motivation, accountability, and alignment with IFC's strategic goals. • Mentor, coach, and develop staff, including junior staff, and guide and incentivize teams to achieve results. • Demonstrate excellent people-management skills, openness to feedback and new ideas, ability to inspire trust, and sensitivity in multicultural environments.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: Investment Analyst - Manufacturing and Services
item.description: Build a career with impact. Working at the World Bank Group (WBG) provides a unique opportunity to help countries solve their greatest development challenges. As one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries, the WBG is a unique partnership of five global institutions dedicated to ending poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, the WBG works with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. The Knowledge Bank is the WBG’s platform for generating, curating, and applying global knowledge to support development impact at scale. It connects operational experience, analytical work, data, and global expertise to inform policy dialogue, country programs, and investments across the World Bank Group. The WBG’s Prosperity Vice Presidency, which includes the Bank’s Manufacturing and Manufacturing Services expertise, works to connect our operational teams with clients by providing public and private sector solutions to expand Manufacturing opportunities in emerging markets. Within this structure, the Manufacturing and Services Solutions and Impact Department (WKPTM) focuses on supporting investments in Manufacturing value chains including but not limited to those in Construction Materials (steel, cement, glass, etc.), Chemicals, Fertilizers, Automotive, Electronics, Batteries, Machinery, Textiles and Apparel, Packaging and other sectors involved in industrial production, value enhancement, and processing. The WKPTM group is looking to hire an Investment Analyst to support the implementation of the Manufacturing and Manufacturing Services sectors’ investments and solutions work. The Investment Analyst will help (i) prepare sector mappings, strategies, and management briefings; (ii) further WBG expertise in Manufacturing through benchmarking, analyzing industry trends, and tracking lessons learned and disseminating the same across the organization; (iii) develop new business, support transactions; and (ii) manage key global client relationships. The Investment Analyst will work closely with sector and team leads, industry specialists and advisors, regional teams, and other partners to support industry and market research. The position will be based in Washington, DC. Some travel may be required. Duties and Accountabilities: The responsibilities of the Investment Analyst will include but are not limited to: Sector Support, Business Development and Knowledge Management : • Provide market research and analysis, including comparator analysis, assessment of market dynamics, benchmarking, and competitiveness; • Prepare sector mapping to identify and target potential global clients and business opportunities; • Research prospective client companies; • Assist in preparing sector strategies and client pitchbooks; and • Contribute to dissemination and exchange of knowledge. Deal Execution and Investment Portfolio : • Collect market data and coordinate with clients to obtain necessary information to support financial, operational and development impact assessments; • Analyze company financial statements and projections, build financial models, and prepare sensitivity analyses; and • With supervision from senior colleagues, coordinate with internal resources (legal, ES G, economists, sector specialists, etc.) for input on relevant investment documents and analyses.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: E T Consultant- Data Scientist Consultant
item.description: Do you want to build a career that is truly worthwhile? Working at the World Bank provides a unique opportunity to help clients address their most pressing development challenges. The World Bank consists of two entities—the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD) and the International Development Association (IDA). It is a global development cooperative owned by 189 member countries. As the world’s largest development bank, it provides loans, guarantees, risk management products, and advisory services to middle-income and creditworthy low-income countries, and coordinates responses to regional and global challenges. For more information, please visit www.worldbank.org. Infrastructure Vertical The World Bank Group (WBG) Infrastructure Vertical provides public and private solutions to operational teams and clients across the WBG to produce scalable impact. Its mandate is to deliver knowledge for impact to support the WBG to achieve its goals in support of our mission and specifically: providing guidance on creating more and better jobs by: (i) supporting foundational infrastructure and human capital, (ii) policy environment, and (iii) enabling and mobilizing private sector capital. With 60 years of experience, the WBG is poised to support the sector becoming an engine of growth and jobs through improving policies and governance, foundational infrastructure and private sector investments. WBG lending in the sector is expected to grow substantially. The Infrastructure VPU’s objectives in driving outcomes include replicating and scaling effective solutions, enhancing thought leadership and innovation, and delivering timely knowledge to client teams. For more information: https://www.worldbank.org/en/topic/infrastructure The Disaster Management, Solutions Impact Unit (KIUSD) supports low and middle-income countries to understand, manage, and reduce their risks from natural hazards and climate change. Within KIUSD, the Global Facility for Disaster Reduction and Recovery (GFDRR) umbrella trust fund is a global partnership that helps low- and middle-income countries better understand and reduce their vulnerability to natural hazards and climate change. GFDRR supports analytics and technical assistance to governments in the most vulnerable countries and communities where it will have the greatest positive impact. As a donor-funded program, GFDRR consults regularly with donors and actively strives to maintain and develop new partnerships for increased impact. For more information: https://www.gfdrr.org/en Please note this position is financed by a Trust Fund and renewal is contingent upon availability of funding, in addition to other factors, including but not limited to business needs, staff performance and skills fit. Job Duties and Responsibilities: The overall objective of the assignment is to develop and apply in-house resilient infrastructure analytics covering exposure, vulnerability, criticality analyses. The Data Scientist will support the Resilient Infrastructure thematic area in GFDRR (RI). The immediate focus is to further develop and apply the “Infrascan” tool, which produces multi-hazard exposure, vulnerability, and risk assessments for infrastructure assets. RI anticipates strong demand for these outputs from World Bank task teams, both for operations and for advisory and advocacy products. The Data Scientist will play a key role in responding to task teams by producing semi-standardized “Infrascan” notes and supporting teams in interpreting and communicating results, including in policy preliminary investment dialogues. On tool development, the Data Scientist will help refine and expand the tool by: adding additional hazards (potentially including drought, extreme heat, and wildfires); increasing automation to make the workflow as close to plug-and-play as feasible across sectors and hazards; and contributing to a bottom-up repository of local datasets covering infrastructure assets, hazards, vulnerability curves, and asset cost estimates. In addition, the Data Scientist will contribute to other RI workstreams and help catalyze collaboration with other GFDRR thematic areas and relevant analytics produced by WBG teams. This includes: (i) running network analysis tools to assess the impacts of hazard-related transport disruptions at urban and inter-regional scales; (ii) advancing the development and analytics for geospatial “predicted employment” layers based on an existing algorithm; (iii) supporting the application of methodologies to generate extreme event damage scenarios for system stress testing; and (iv) contributing to other RI analytics, including work on cost–benefit ratios of resilience investments and the identification of critical elements required to maintain infrastructure services. The Data Scientist will be a core member of the RI, providing analytics to support World Bank regional teams and strengthen the systematic integration of resilience into infrastructure-related policy and investment operations, including: • Take a leading role in the development and application of geospatial and other quantitative tools within RI. • Support the development and delivery of knowledge products and operational toolkits to enhance capacity of task teams to deliver operations related to resilient infrastructure, including assessing impacts and identifying mitigation measures for climate-related hazards and geohazards. • Support task teams in communicating results from RI tool applications through presentations, training, talking points, and concise summaries in multiple formats. • Coordinate with key partners inside and outside the WBG that manage or develop geospatial tools. • Contribute to program deliverables, including preparation of GFDRR communication and dissemination materials, results monitoring, reporting, budgeting, and branding. • Deliver presentations and workshops in a variety of settings, including with task teams, government counterparts, and internal and external events. • Carry out other tasks, as needed, to support the overall objective of the position.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: E T Consultant - Senior Infrastructure Consultant
item.description: Do you want to build a career that is truly worthwhile? Working at the World Bank provides a unique opportunity for you to help our clients solve their greatest development challenges. The World Bank consists of two entities – the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD) and the International Development Association (IDA). It is a global development cooperative owned by 189 member countries. As the largest development bank in the world, the World Bank provides loans, guarantees, risk management products, and advisory services to middle-income and creditworthy low-income countries, and coordinates responses to regional and global challenges. For more information, please visit www.worldbank.org . Infrastructure Vertical The WBG Infrastructure Vertical provides public and private solutions to operational teams and clients across the WBG to produce scalable impact. Its mandate is to deliver knowledge for impact to support the WBG to achieve its goals in support of our mission and specifically: providing guidance on creating more and better jobs by: (i) supporting foundational infrastructure and human capital, (ii) policy environment, and (iii) enabling and mobilizing private sector capital. With 60 years of experience, the WBG is poised to support the sector becoming an engine of growth and jobs through improving policies and governance, foundational infrastructure and private sector investments. The WBG lending in the sector is expected to grow substantially. The Infrastructure VPU’s objectives in driving outcomes include replicating and scaling effective solutions, enhancing thought leadership and innovation, and delivering timely knowledge to client teams. For more information: https://www.worldbank.org/en/topic/infrastructure The Disaster Management, Solutions Impact Unit (KIUSD) supports low and middle-income countries to understand, manage, and reduce their risks from natural hazards and climate change. Within KIUSD, the Global Facility for Disaster Reduction and Recovery (GFDRR) umbrella trust fund is a global partnership that helps low- and middle-income countries better understand and reduce their vulnerability to natural hazards and climate change. GFDRR supports analytics and technical assistance to governments in the most vulnerable countries and communities where it will have the greatest positive impact. As a donor-funded program, GFDRR consults regularly with donors and actively strives to maintain and develop new partnerships for increased impact. For more information: https://www.gfdrr.org/en Please note this position is financed by a Trust Fund and renewal is contingent upon availability of funding, in addition to other factors, including but not limited to business needs, staff performance and skills fit. Job Duties and Responsibilities: The Senior Infrastructure Consultant will serve as a global technical advisor within KIUSD- Resilient Infrastructure Thematic Area, providing strategic and operational support to World Bank regional teams to strengthen the systematic integration of resilience into infrastructure-related policies, investment operations, and analytical work, including: • Support the development of knowledge products and operational toolkit to enhance capacity of task teams to deliver operations related to resilient infrastructure, assessing the impact and identifying mitigation measures to climate-related hazards and geohazards, such as soil erosion and landslides. • Support the coherent integration of resilience considerations across the policy–investment–analytics continuum, ensuring alignment between upstream diagnostics, design standards, investment design, and implementation support. • Provide advice on resilience engineering standards, performance metrics, indicators, and results frameworks in infrastructure operations. • As part of country activities, provide technical advice on rapid diagnostics of policies and design of resilient infrastructure, as well as exposure, vulnerability, risk and post-disaster assessments. • Provide targeted, demand-driven advisory support to regional task teams and client counterparts, focusing on the preparation and implementation of lending operation projects that involve resilient infrastructure activities, focusing on roads and drainage. Activities include but are not limited to supporting the formulation of terms of reference, technical review of reports and due diligence of the project scoping, preparation, or implementation. • Liaise with stakeholders across government, contractors, and development partners as part of country activities and technical assistance. • Oversee the implementation of TA activities including but not limited to planning, budgeting, preparation of terms of references, quality control, supervision of external consultants and technical specialists work as well as developing and reviewing Trust Fund Proposals, contributing to Trust Funds reporting, and developing of terms of reference. • Contribute and support capacity building activities for WBG Task Teams and client countries’ teams involved in resilient infrastructure projects. • Participate in field missions and provide technical input and guidance to client counterparts as required by operational and analytical task team leaders. • Contribute to program deliverables, including preparation of GFDRR communication and dissemination materials, results monitoring, reporting, budgeting, and branding. • Carry out other tasks relevant to support the overall objective of the position.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: (Associate) Investment Officer - Energy
item.description: Build a career with impact. Working at the World Bank Group (WBG) provides a unique opportunity to help countries solve their greatest development challenges. As one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries, the WBG is a unique partnership of five global institutions dedicated to ending poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, the WBG works with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. The World Bank’s Infrastructure Vice Presidency, part of the WBG Knowledge Bank, provides investment and advisory services for private and public sector clients across energy, mining and metals, transport, waste, and tourism sectors, and further mobilizes private sector capital from institutional investors and other financial institutions. It acts as a strategic partner to the private sector, leveraging its financial resources, deep-seated global expertise, and ability to mitigate risk and mobilize capital to foster infrastructure development that opens markets, drives economic growth, creates jobs and improves living standards in emerging markets in the development of a wide array of infrastructure sectors. The Energy Investments, Solutions Impact team (KIESI) within the WBG Infrastructure Vice Presidency seeks to accelerate critical private sector investments in energy infrastructure, including by leveraging the Knowledge Bank resources to promote initiatives and innovative financing products. Our team is united by core values of Impact, Integrity, Respect, Teamwork, and Innovation. Together, we tackle the world’s toughest challenges, and advance social and economic inclusion. KIESI is looking to recruit an (Associate) Investment Officer to contribute to the Energy Department’s priorities comprising development and dissemination of sector strategy and expertise, market creation and innovation, as well as supporting WBG decision-making processes for new energy investments, management of key global client relationships, business development, and transaction execution. D uties and Accountabilities • Work closely with other members of the team and the Knowledge Bank to support IFC investment teams globally in structuring debt, equity, and blended finance instruments. • Collaborate with interdisciplinary Knowledge Bank teams to support IFC's investment decision making process by promoting best practices and sharing global investment expertise. • Contribute to knowledge-sharing initiatives and provide insights on market trends, risks, and opportunities in the energy sector to inform WBG strategic decision-making. • Support the development and implementation of WBG strategies, as well as innovative financing solutions and initiatives to address market gaps and promote private sector participation in selected energy segments. • From time to time, as member of project teams, contribute to execution of new IFC investment transactions including preparing investment proposals, conducting due diligence as well as detailed financial, market, and risk analyses. • Work with Global relationship managers to build and maintain strong relationships with clients, industry stakeholders, and co-investors to identify and develop a robust pipeline of high-impact investment opportunities.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: Senior Investment Officer - Manufacturing, Agribusiness & Services (MAS) Europe, Upstream & Advisory
item.description: Build a career with impact. Working at the World Bank Group (WBG) provides a unique opportunity to help countries solve their greatest development challenges. As one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries, the WBG is a unique partnership of five global institutions dedicated to ending poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, the WBG works with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. The International Finance Corporation (IFC), a member of the World Bank Group, is the largest global development institution focused on the private sector in emerging markets. We work in more than 100 countries, using our capital, expertise, and influence to create markets and opportunities in developing countries. In fiscal year 2025, IFC committed a record $71.7 billion to private companies and financial institutions in developing countries, leveraging private sector solutions, and mobilizing private capital to create a world free of poverty on a livable planet. For more information, visit www.ifc.org. Working at the WBG provides a unique opportunity to help countries solve their greatest development challenges. Through its five global institutions — IBRD, IDA, IFC, MIGA and ICSID the WBG works with public and private sector partners to address the world's most urgent challenges. At the core of this effort is a shared mission: creating jobs that lift people out of poverty and contribute to a world free of poverty on a livable planet. With the private sector accounting for approximately 90% of jobs in developing countries, IFC’s work is central to the World Bank Group’s jobs agenda. Through Upstream and Advisory IFC leverages WBG expertise, tools and solutions to address market barriers, support reforms, and develop impactful and investable opportunities in order to mobilize private capital and create jobs across sectors with significant growth and employment potential, such as foundational infrastructure, manufacturing, agribusiness, healthcare and tourism. MAS EUR Upstream Advisory is seeking a Senior Investment Officer (SIO), who will also serve as IFC’s Hub Lead for Poland, to drive strategic origination and impactful Upstream Advisory engagements across the MAS sectors in the Europe region. The Senior Investment Officer will lead proactive business development, support design and delivery One WBG initiatives that contextualize scalable WBG solutions to deliver on Country Partnership Framework objectives and convert early-stage engagements into a strong pipeline of bankable investments. Based in Warsaw, the Senior Investment Officer will serve as IFC’s Hub Lead for Poland — representing IFC in the local market, coordinating and championing MAS business development and Upstream activities in Poland, and building a strong pipeline of bankable investments in support of the Country Partnership Framework, while contributing to and helping shape the broader Europe regional Upstream and business development program. The role will also support IFC’s engagement in Ukraine, including private-sector origination and Upstream work that helps mobilize capital for the country’s recovery and reconstruction, and will explore the role Poland can play as a platform for reconstruction-linked investment, supply chains, and regional integration. Duties and Accountabilities: • Serve as IFC’s Hub Lead for Poland: setting MAS strategic priorities in the country, shaping the WBG country strategy and business plan to drive growth in MAS sectors, representing MAS in the local market, contributing to joint WBG engagements, serving as the primary point of contact for clients, partners and WBG counterparts, and leading and mentoring the local team. • Deliver at least five (5) bankable mandates annually for high-impact investments with >5x private capital mobilization potential, contributing to the achievement of the WBG Scorecard outcome indicators in MAS sectors in Poland and CEE. • Build and manage senior-level relationships with clients, private sector partners, financial institutions, multilaterals, and governments to originate and advance investment opportunities. • Lead MAS business development in Poland, building a strong, high-quality pipeline of bankable investments aligned with the Country Partnership Framework, while supporting and contributing to the broader Europe regional program. • Support IFC’s engagement across CEE, including investment, Upstream, and Advisory activities that mobilize private capital and support regional growth, recovery and reconstruction. • Identify and develop opportunities for Poland to play a role in Ukraine’s reconstruction — including cross-border investment, supply chains, and platforms that connect Polish and regional private-sector capacity to reconstruction needs. • Design and deliver Upstream engagements focused on originating bankable investment opportunities that convert WBG-supported reforms into private sector investments and structuring innovative financing solutions to crowd in private capital. • Design and lead the delivery Advisory engagements that strengthen private sector competitiveness, build market opportunities, and support the implementation of WBG priorities through scalable, private sector-led solutions. • Leverage market intelligence and sector insights to shape strategic priorities, identify emerging investment opportunities, and enhance the origination and structuring of high-impact transactions. • Collaborate across teams and regions to develop sector/country strategies, deliver integrated solutions, and maximize development impact of IFC investments in emerging Europe. • Mentor, coach, and develop junior team members, fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, accountability and excellence.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group
