item.title: Policy Analyst - Economist - Social economy and social innovation
item.description: Main Responsibilities Research, analysis and drafting Design, lead, and contribute to high-impact analytical and policy-oriented research and case studies on the social economy and social entrepreneurship, including framework conditions, sectoral applications in care, housing, energy, and retail, skills and employment strategies, integration of social economy actors in supply chains, data, digitalisation, AI, productivity and competitiveness. Analyse international evidence and data to inform policy analysis on the Unit’s topics (social economy, social entrepreneurship and social innovation) in OECD member countries and beyond. Draft and edit high-quality evidence-based assessments and tailored recommendations in substantive policy papers, case studies, and chapters in reviews or thematic reports, designed for policymakers and interested audience. Prepare other high-quality documents, including blogs, seminar proceedings, and policy messages based on research and relevant published material. Organise and summarise complex material in a clear manner for specialist and non-specialist audiences. Keep abreast of emerging research, exploratory work and evolving policies on local economic development, emanating from governments, other international organisations, non-governmental organisations, academia and the private sector. Strategic Leadership and Project Management Provide strategic input into the Committee on Local Employment and Economic Developpement (DC LEED), Programme of Work and Budget (PWB), and contribute to its implementation. Conduct and contribute to country level projects involving both qualitative and quantitative research, policy reviews, peer learning activities, and capacity building. Manage staff and consultants, including junior analysts, consultants, and external experts; ensure the delivery of quality outputs on time and within budget. Work collaboratively with other divisions and directorates on horizontal initiatives. Oversee the planning, execution and delivery of meetings and workshops on topics related to social economy and social entrepreneurship. Contribute to and oversee the preparation of presentations, speeches, briefings, press releases and other communication materials. Project management and fundraising Contribute actively to the management of projects, including ensuring the timely delivery of high-quality outputs. Actively support the Division’s and CFE’s fundraising efforts, including drafting project proposals and strengthening networks and engagement with potential donors as required. Supervise the work of junior staff, including interns, external and internal consultants as needed. Co-ordination, representation and liaison Support the activities of the Social Economy Unit, including organising webinars and in-person events, as well as contributing content to the social-economy online toolkit . Organise other project-related meetings and events, such as seminars, roundtables and workshops, as needed. Establish and maintain contacts with high-level representatives in relevant ministries and government agencies, as well as intergovernmental organisations and relevant stakeholders. Represent the OECD Secretariat at international conferences and contribute to raising the profile and disseminating LEED and CFE work at local, national and international levels. Collaborate with other Divisions and OECD directorates actively contributing to horizontal and multidisciplinary projects and outputs as needed. Other duties Provide general support to the activities of the Division as required, in particular as requested by LESI management and the Director’s office.
item.dc:creator: OECD

item.title: Analyste des politiques - Économiste – Économie sociale et innovation sociale
item.description: Responsabilités principales Recherche, analyse et rédaction Concevoir, diriger et contribuer à des recherches analytiques et politiques à fort impact, ainsi qu’à des études de cas sur l’économie sociale et l’entrepreneuriat social, incluant les conditions cadres, les applications sectorielles dans les domaines du soin, du logement, de l’énergie et du commerce de détail, les stratégies de compétences et d’emploi, l’intégration des acteurs de l’économie sociale dans les chaînes de valeur, les données, la digitalisation, l’IA, la productivité et la compétitivité. Analyser les données internationales pour éclairer l’analyse des politiques sur les thèmes de l’Unité (économie sociale, entrepreneuriat social et innovation sociale) dans les pays membres de l’OCDE et au-delà. Rédiger et éditer des évaluations basées sur des preuves de haute qualité et des recommandations adaptées dans des documents politiques, des études de cas et des chapitres de revues ou de rapports thématiques, destinés aux décideurs et à un public intéressé. Préparer d’autres documents de qualité, tels que des blogs, des comptes rendus de séminaires et des messages politiques, basés sur la recherche et les publications pertinentes. Organiser et résumer des matériaux complexes de manière claire pour un public spécialisé et non spécialisé. Se tenir au courant des recherches émergentes, des travaux exploratoires et des politiques évolutives sur le développement économique local, émanant des gouvernements, d’autres organisations internationales, d’ONG, du monde universitaire et du secteur privé. Leadership stratégique et gestion de projets Contribuer au Comité de l’emploi local et le développement économique (DC LEED), au Programme de travail et au Budget (PWB), et à leur mise en œuvre. Mener et contribuer à des projets au niveau national impliquant des recherches qualitatives et quantitatives, des revues de politiques, des activités d’apprentissage par les pairs et le renforcement des capacités. Gérer le personnel et les consultants, y compris les analystes juniors, consultants et experts externes ; assurer la livraison de résultats de qualité dans les délais et dans le respect du budget. Collaborer avec d’autres divisions et directions sur des initiatives transversales. Superviser la planification, l’organisation et la réalisation de réunions et d’ateliers sur des thèmes liés à l’économie sociale et à l’entrepreneuriat social. Contribuer à la préparation et superviser la réalisation de présentations, discours, briefings, communiqués de presse et autres supports de communication. Gestion de projets et collecte de fonds Contribuer activement à la gestion des projets, en veillant à la livraison en temps voulu de résultats de haute qualité. Soutenir activement les efforts de fundraising de la Division et du CFE, notamment en rédigeant des propositions et en renforçant les réseaux et l’engagement avec les donateurs potentiels, selon les besoins. Superviser le travail du personnel junior, y compris les stagiaires, consultants externes et internes, selon les besoins. Coordination, représentation et liaison Appuyer les activités de l’Unité économie sociale et innovation, notamment en organisant des webinaires et des événements en présentiel, ainsi qu’en contribuant au contenu de la boîte à outils en ligne sur l’économie sociale . Organiser d’autres réunions et événements liés aux projets, tels que des séminaires, tables rondes et ateliers, selon les besoins. Établir et maintenir des contacts avec des représentants de haut niveau dans les ministères, agences gouvernementales, organisations intergouvernementales et parties prenantes pertinentes. Représenter le Secrétariat de l’OCDE lors de conférences internationales et contribuer à accroître la visibilité et la diffusion des travaux du LEED et du CFE à l’échelle locale, nationale et internationale. Collaborer activement avec d’autres divisions et directions de l’OCDE pour contribuer à des projets et résultats transversaux et pluridisciplinaires selon les besoins. Autres tâches Fournir un soutien général aux activités de la Division selon les besoins, en particulier à la demande de la direction de LESI et du bureau du Directeur.
item.dc:creator: OECD

item.title: Product Engineer
item.description: Product discovery and functional design Lead product discovery with ISRP business units and stakeholders by facilitating workshops, conducting structured requirements gathering and modelling user journeys for core business applications in areas such as pension administration, compensation and benefits, surveys, and legal and document services. Author functional specifications and acceptance criteria, and drive them through to stakeholder sign-off and user acceptance testing. Analyse existing and legacy applications to recover and document business rules in preparation for their progressive replacement, and lead as a first priority the functional design of the rebuild of the IFI portal (International Financial Institutions). Maintain a structured and transparent product backlog informed by user feedback and business priorities, facilitate the intake and prioritisation of development requests across the Unit’s portfolio, and support fellow developers in structuring and managing their backlogs. Design, development and delivery Design and deliver user-centred and accessible interfaces for ISRP web applications and future digital services, in line with recognised accessibility standards. Develop, test and ship full-stack web applications on a modern TypeScript stack, in line with OECD security requirements, controls and development standards. Contribute to the progressive modernisation of the application portfolio through the replacement of end-of-life applications, with further rebuild projects to follow. Co-ordinate with internal and external service providers on integrations, environment provisioning and handovers. Operations, knowledge and continuous improvement Participate in the operational running of ISRP digital services on the basis of documented operating procedures, including scheduled application releases, monthly updates of application data and content, yearly campaigns to gather pension-related information and documents, and responses to user service requests. Continuously improve and automate these operating procedures to strengthen service reliability, repeatability and operational resilience. Establish and maintain the team’s knowledge infrastructure, including documentation standards, a central knowledge base, operating procedures and onboarding paths, to support auditability, service continuity and effective cross-coverage. Define, measure and report service and product key performance indicators, and prepare analyses and presentation materials to support management reporting and decision-making. Identify and propose improvements to workflows and tooling, and champion shared practices across the Service’s IT activities to contribute to a culture of continuous learning and knowledge-sharing.
item.dc:creator: OECD

item.title: Ingénieur(e) web orienté(e) produit
item.description: Découverte produit et conception fonctionnelle Piloter les activités de découverte produit avec les unités métier et les parties prenantes du SIRP en animant des ateliers, en recueillant les besoins de manière structurée et en modélisant les parcours utilisateurs pour les applications métier essentielles, notamment dans les domaines de l’administration des pensions, des rémunérations et avantages, des enquêtes et des services juridiques et documentaires. Rédiger les spécifications fonctionnelles et les critères d’acceptation, puis les conduire jusqu’à leur validation par les parties prenantes et aux tests d’acceptation utilisateurs. Analyser les applications existantes afin de reconstituer et documenter les règles métier en vue de leur remplacement progressif, et conduire en priorité la conception fonctionnelle de la refonte du portail IFI (Institutions financières internationales). Maintenir un backlog produit structuré et transparent fondé sur les retours utilisateurs et les priorités métier, faciliter la réception et la priorisation des demandes de développement au sein du portefeuille de l’Unité et accompagner les autres développeurs dans la gestion de leurs backlogs. Conception, développement et livraison Concevoir et livrer des interfaces accessibles et centrées sur l’utilisateur pour les applications web du SIRP et les futurs services numériques, conformément aux normes reconnues d’accessibilité. Développer, tester et mettre en production, du front-end au back-end, des applications web full stack sur une pile technologique moderne fondée sur TypeScript. dans le respect des exigences de sécurité, des contrôles et des standards de développement de l’OCDE. Contribuer à la modernisation progressive du portefeuille applicatif en remplaçant les applications arrivées en fin de vie, d’autres projets de refonte devant suivre. Coordonner les activités avec les prestataires internes et externes concernant les intégrations, la mise à disposition des environnements et les transferts de responsabilité. Exploitation, gestion des connaissances et amélioration continue Participer à l’exploitation des services numériques du SIRP conformément aux procédures documentées, notamment les mises en production planifiées, les mises à jour mensuelles des données et contenus applicatifs, les campagnes annuelles de collecte d’informations et de documents relatifs aux pensions, ainsi que le traitement des demandes de support utilisateurs. Améliorer et automatiser en permanence ces procédures opérationnelles afin de renforcer la fiabilité, la reproductibilité et la résilience des services. Mettre en place et maintenir l’infrastructure de gestion des connaissances de l’équipe, notamment les normes documentaires, une base de connaissances centralisée, les procédures d’exploitation et les parcours d’intégration, afin de garantir la traçabilité, la continuité des services et la polyvalence des équipes. Définir, mesurer et communiquer les indicateurs clés de performance des services et produits, et préparer des analyses et présentations à l’appui du reporting et de la prise de décision. Identifier et proposer des améliorations des processus et des outils, et promouvoir les bonnes pratiques communes au sein des activités informatiques du Service afin de favoriser une culture d’apprentissage continu et de partage des connaissances.
item.dc:creator: OECD

item.title: E T Consultant
item.description: Do you want to build a career that is truly worthwhile? The World Bank Group is one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries; a unique global partnership of five institutions dedicated to ending extreme poverty and promoting shared prosperity. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, we work with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. For more information, www.worldbank.org E astern and Southern Africa Regional Context The Eastern and Southern Africa (AFE) region is diverse, spanning low-income and lower-middle-income countries and a number of upper-middle-income borrowers, with wide variation in health outcomes, fiscal space, and institutional capacity. Many countries face a high and evolving burden of disease alongside persistent inequities in access and quality, while also managing recurrent shocks, including climate-related hazards, epidemics, and food and fuel price volatility. In this context, the World Bank supports governments through analytics, policy dialogue, and financing to strengthen human capital and build more resilient health systems and essential public health functions. Through its Health, Nutrition, and Population (HNP) Global Practice, the World Bank supports countries’ efforts towards achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) and providing quality, affordable, equity-focused health services to everyone regardless of their ability to pay by strengthening primary health care systems and reducing the financial risks associated with ill-health. Some of the ongoing health and nutrition challenges in the AFE region include: • Triple burden of disease and epidemiological transition: a continuing burden of communicable diseases (including HIV, TB, malaria and outbreaks), persistently high maternal, newborn and child mortality in many settings, and a rapidly growing burden of NCDs and injuries—often coexisting within the same populations and health systems. • Health system constraints and inequities: persistent gaps in access to quality essential services, especially for rural, remote, and vulnerable populations, driven by constraints in the health workforce, medicines and supply chains, service readiness, governance and accountability, and limited fiscal space and financial protection for households. • Nutrition and food insecurity: high levels of child stunting and micronutrient deficiencies in several countries, alongside rising overweight/obesity, compounded by food price shocks and climate variability that affect diets and worsen vulnerability throughout the life course. Summary of Botswana and Southern Africa Health Portfolio The World Bank’s health portfolio in Botswana combines investment financing with targeted technical assistance (TA) and analytics to advance the Government’s priorities on UHC, primary health care revitalization, and health security. The portfolio is anchored by the Botswana Health Emergency Preparedness, Response and Resilience (HEPRR) Project, which supports preparedness and response; strengthened surveillance, laboratory, and emergency management systems; and investments in core enablers, including digital public health infrastructure and availability of quality data. In parallel, the World Bank provides TA supporting sector planning and governance such as development of the Health Sector Strategic Plan, digital health and data use (interoperability, registries, and routine data quality and use), and improvements in supply chain performance and regulatory systems for health products. This work is complemented by analytics to inform reforms and investments, including on health financing and efficiency, equity, and service delivery performance to strengthen human capital and system resilience. Across southern Africa, the World Bank’s health engagement in South Africa, Namibia, Eswatini, and Lesotho combines investment lending with analytical and advisory services to strengthen primary health care, improve quality of care, and build more resilient health systems. In Lesotho, the portfolio includes an active health and nutrition system strengthening project focused on expanding coverage and improving the quality of essential maternal and child health, nutrition, and NCD services, alongside reforms to strengthen stewardship and service delivery readiness. There are opportunities to support the preparation of new operations and enhance the scope of TA and advisory work in southern Africa. In South Africa and Namibia, support is anchored in policy dialogue and analytics, including public expenditure and efficiency work, to inform reforms that expand effective coverage, reduce inequities, and improve the sustainability of health financing. Objectives and Scope of Work In light of the growing health portfolio, the HAEH2 team seeks an Extended-Term Consultant (ETC) with experience as a Health Specialist/Economist to provide day-to-day technical and operational support to HNP task teams and Government counterparts, primarily in Botswana and, as needed, across the southern Africa portfolio. The scope of work includes: • Provide technical and operational support to the health portfolio in Botswana. The ETC will support implementation of the Botswana health portfolio (including the HEPRR project) by monitoring progress against the results framework (including support to M E, data quality, and results reporting) and follow-up on agreed actions; supporting planning and delivery of implementation support missions; and contributing to key operational deliverables (aide memoires, management briefs, and client-facing communications). The ETC will help convene consultations with the Ministry of Health and other stakeholders, monitor progress, and coordinate with fiduciary, safeguards, and technical colleagues to ensure timely, integrated support. • Help ensure close coordination with government, development partners, and the CMU and World Bank teams across other sectors in Botswana. The ETC will facilitate coordination and information flow across the Ministry of Health and other relevant agencies, development partners, and World Bank teams (including the Country Management Unit and colleagues in other sectors) to align priorities, timelines, and implementation support. The ETC will play a pivotal role in providing strategic sectoral guidance to the CMU and provide key inputs into country management reviews and documents. • Support policy dialogue with the Ministry of Health, relevant national institutions, and other stakeholders. The ETC will support policy dialogue by preparing briefs, talking points, and presentations, synthesizing implementation experience, sector analytics, and international lessons. The ETC will help translate discussions into clear decisions and follow-up actions to inform priority reforms in primary health care, health security, health financing, and other system strengthening areas. • Provide technical and operational support to the health portfolio in southern Africa, as needed. The ETC will support task teams in South Africa, Namibia, Eswatini, and Lesotho, including assistance with preparation and follow-up of missions and key deliverables, and contributions to selected analytical and advisory work (e.g., service delivery performance, health financing and efficiency, and health security). Duties and Accountabilities: The ETC’s key tasks and activities will include but are not limited to: • Support implementation support for HEPRR and other Botswana portfolio activities, including monitoring implementation and results; consolidating inputs across technical, fiduciary and ESF colleagues; and proactively following up with counterparts on agreed actions. • Contribute to the planning, preparation, and follow-up of missions, technical consultations, and key portfolio engagements, including drafting agenda/talking points, preparing briefing packages, and drafting aide memoires and progress reports, and management updates as requested by Task Team Leaders. • Support monitoring and evaluation (M E) and results reporting for the portfolio, including maintaining an M E implementation plan, supporting updates to the results framework and indicator definitions as needed, compiling and quality-checking data and narrative inputs for progress reporting. • Provide technical inputs to project implementation, including review of technical specifications and Terms of References for goods and services (e.g., medical and laboratory equipment, digital systems, and technical assistance), and advise on alignment with good practice, user requirements, and World Bank procurement principles (in coordination with procurement specialists). • Support stakeholder coordination across Government, development partners, and World Bank teams (including the CMU and other sectors) by preparing coordination notes, documenting decisions, and escalating issues requiring management attention. • Contribute to selected analytical and advisory activities (ASA), including literature and data review, drafting sections of technical notes and reports, and supporting quality assurance to ensure analytical rigor and consistency of messaging. • As needed, provide operational and analytical support to the southern Africa health portfolio (South Africa, Namibia, Eswatini, and Lesotho). • Maintain well-organized records of key documents and correspondence; support timely sharing of information within task teams; and contribute to knowledge management by capturing lessons learned and good practices.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: Economist
item.description: Do you want to build a career that is truly worthwhile? The World Bank Group is one of the largest sources of funding and knowledge for developing countries; a unique global partnership of five institutions dedicated to ending extreme poverty and promoting shared prosperity. With 189 member countries and more than 120 offices worldwide, we work with public and private sector partners, investing in groundbreaking projects and using data, research, and technology to develop solutions to the most urgent global challenges. For more information, visit www.worldbank.org Global practice for Fiscal Policy and Growth (FPG) The Fiscal Policy and Growth (FPG) Department leads the World Bank Group’s policy dialogue and engagement with countries to strengthen macro‑fiscal foundations, including debt sustainability, debt management, and domestic resource mobilization, and to accelerate job and inclusive growth. FPG links the macroeconomic dialogue with programs focused on expanding the role of the private sector in economic development. In our day-to-day engagement with countries, FPG supports Ministries of Finance, Planning, Economy, Industry and Trade, and Production in their pursuit of reform agendas conducive to strengthening job creation through inclusive growth led by the private sector. For more information: https://www.worldbank.org/ext/en/topic/fiscal-policy-and-growth Background and General description The FPG unit for Southern Africa and East Africa (EAEM2) is looking for an Economist to be based in Luanda, Angola. The selected candidate will contribute substantially to analytical work and lending operations. She or he will report to the FPG Practice Manager covering the EAEM2 unit, which comprises 14 countries over three Country Management Units: AECS1 (South Africa, Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Namibia), AECS2 (Mozambique, Comoros, Madagascar, Mauritius, Seychelles), and AECC2 (Angola, DRC, São Tomé and Príncipe, and Burundi). The unit has recently seen a significant increase in work program including through Development Policy Operations (DPOs) in countries that had previously not been borrowing. The unit has a strong focus on promoting prudent macro-fiscal policies, structural reforms, inclusive growth, and job creation. The World Bank's Fiscal Policy and Growth (FPG) Global Practice has been a key partner to the Government of Angola in supporting the country's transition from an oil-dependent economy toward a more diversified, inclusive, and private sector-led growth model. Over the past decade, FPG's engagement has included successive programmatic Development Policy Financing operations—such as the Growth and Inclusion DPF series and, most recently, the Green, Resilient, Inclusive Growth, and Diversification DPL series, which included an innovative debt-for-development swap. These operations have supported reforms in fiscal consolidation and resilience, fiscal transparency, and state-owned enterprise governance and restructuring. The work program is underpinned by rigorous analytical products, including the Angola Public Finance Review, the Country Economic Memorandum and annual Economic updates. As a an Economist for EAEM2, you will be expected to contribute to the country team covering Angola and São Tomé and Príncipe, as well as the regional FPG team working on other countries as needed. The Country Economist will be based in Luanda and work closely with the Country Manager, Senior Country Economist and Lead Economist and report to the FPG Practice Manager covering Angola and São Tomé and Príncipe. You will contribute to ongoing efforts to help Angola strengthen fiscal and debt sustainability, expand non-oil revenue mobilization, and build the macroeconomic foundations needed for sustained, inclusive growth and economic diversification. The Country Economist will: • Contribute to FPG Advisory Services and Analytics (ASA) in the areas of macroeconomic and fiscal management, macro modelling and data work, and public finance; • Contribute to the design of the operational and analytical work in alignment with the job creation, economic growth and poverty alleviation objectives of the country; • Serve as a task leader or a team member for country-specific financing operations and/or analytical and advisory services; • Support the Country Management Unit (CMU) in the implementation of the World Bank's program in Angola. • Establish and develop strong partnerships with other bilateral and multilateral agencies, increasing cooperation and leveraging Bank resources in analytical and operational work; • Contribute to the dissemination of the Bank’s knowledge products to a wider audience, including civil society, academia, and other key stakeholders. • Collect, analyze and maintain economic and financial data and information related to Angola’s economy, and communicate findings in concise and clear form. • Maintain macroeconomic databases and regularly prepare updated medium-term macroeconomic projections. Use/run economic/financial models and toolkits using existing software packages, or World Bank's own models;
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group

item.title: Upstream & Advisory Manager Manufacturing & Services (M&S) - Africa
item.description: IFC — a member of the World Bank Group — is the largest global development institution focused on the private sector in emerging markets. We work in more than 100 countries, using our capital, expertise, and influence to create markets and opportunities in developing countries. In fiscal year 2025, IFC committed a record $71 billion to private companies and financial institutions in developing countries, leveraging private sector solutions, and mobilizing private capital to create a world free of poverty on a livable planet. For more information, visit www.ifc.org . IFC is seeking an Upstream Advisory Manager for its Manufacturing Services (M S) department in Africa. The Manager will be responsible for IFC's efforts to enable investment opportunities, enhance investment outcomes, improve investment conditions and deliver development impact through upstream and advisory interventions across Manufacturing Services sectors in Africa. The role will support IFC's strategic objectives under IFC 3.0 by helping create markets, develop investable opportunities, strengthen private sector participation, and advance impactful sector initiatives across the region. The Manager will serve as a member of the Africa M S Management Team and will work closely with Regional Industry Managers, Joint Country Representatives, Global Industry teams, World Bank counterparts and Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (MIGA) to formulate market creation strategies, scale development impact and support the generation of investment opportunities. The Manager will also ensure that upstream and advisory interventions are designed from the outset to support upstream mobilization, including by crowding in partners, sponsors, DFIs, donors and private capital around priority market creation initiatives. The Manager will oversee a joint Upstream and Advisory team and ensure coordinated delivery across the continuum of IFC operations, including market creation, project development, client preparation, investment support, and portfolio value creation. Duties and Accountabilities People and Resource Responsibilities • Lead and manage the Upstream Advisory unit and ensure the team is high-performing, collaborative, and aligned with IFC's strategic priorities. • Mentor and develop staff and promote opportunities for professional growth, mobility and continued learning. • Actively contribute as a member of the Africa M S Management Team. • Strengthen collaboration among upstream, advisory and investment teams to maximize development impact and business outcomes. • Manage resources, budgets, and donor funding efficiently to maximize operational effectiveness. Strategic Responsibilities • Formulate and implement upstream and advisory strategies for the Manufacturing Services sectors in Africa in coordination with the Regional Industry Director, Regional Industry Managers, WBG Country Representatives and Global Industry teams. • Support implementation of IFC country business plans, sector strategies and World Bank Group priorities through upstream and advisory interventions. • Lead market creation efforts that expand private sector participation and unlock new opportunities for investment. • Identify upstream opportunities with strong potential to mobilize partners, sponsors, donors, DFIs and private capital in support of IFC's market creation and investment objectives. • Embed upstream mobilization considerations into sector strategies, country business plans and priority initiatives, including tourism, gas-for-power, manufacturing competitiveness, value chain development and other high-impact sectors. • Develop scalable interventions, including job creation, productivity enhancement, competitiveness, inclusion, climate outcomes, and economic transformation. • Advance strategic regional initiatives in tourism, gas-for-power, manufacturing competitiveness, value chain development, and other priority sectors. • Leverage IFC's global sector expertise, product innovation and lessons learned to inform strategy, project design and market creation efforts. • Collaborate closely with Global Industry teams to identify emerging trends, replicate successful business models and adapt global best practices to African markets. Business Responsibilities • Enable investment opportunities by incubating high-impact projects, developing new markets, and preparing clients for investment. • Lead upstream mobilization efforts by identifying, structuring and advancing opportunities that can attract co-investors, sponsors, donors, DFIs and other partners around IFC-led market creation initiatives. • Work with Regional Industry Managers, investment teams and partnerships colleagues to translate upstream engagements into investable opportunities with clear pathways for capital mobilization. • Support the development of scalable platforms, programmatic approaches and partnership models that can mobilize resources and private sector participation beyond individual transactions. • Enhance investment outcomes through advisory and upstream interventions that improve development impact, operational performance, and sustainability. • Improve investment conditions by supporting sector reforms, market development activities, and private sector participation. • Deliver measurable development impact through upstream and advisory interventions. • Support initiatives that contribute to job creation, economic inclusion, industrial development, private sector competitiveness, and climate resilience. • Advance strategic initiatives in tourism, gas-for-power and other high-impact sectors that can catalyze economic growth and private investment. • Work closely with Regional Industry Managers and investment teams to improve conversion of upstream opportunities into investment transactions. • Track upstream mobilization outcomes, including partner commitments, donor resources, co-financing opportunities and private capital enabled through upstream and advisory work. • Ensure a high standard of project design, implementation, monitoring and development effectiveness across the portfolio. Client and Stakeholder Responsibilities • Serve as a principal focal point for collaboration with IBRD, MIGA, Joint Country Representatives and other World Bank Group partners on Manufacturing Services market creation initiatives. • Build and maintain strategic relationships with governments, regulators, sponsors, investors, industry associations, development partners and private sector stakeholders. • Convene and mobilize public and private stakeholders around priority sector platforms and upstream initiatives, with a focus on unlocking investment and scaling development impact. • Convene relevant stakeholders to address market constraints and unlock investment opportunities. • Foster integrated World Bank Group solutions that combine policy reform, advisory support and investment opportunities. Knowledge Capture and Management • Identify game-changing trends, technologies and business models with potential for high development impact across the Manufacturing Services sectors. • Serve as a bridge between Africa operations and IFC's global knowledge platform, ensuring that global expertise, sector insights and innovative practices are effectively applied to local market creation efforts. • Contribute African market insights and operational lessons to IFC's global knowledge agenda. • Strengthen IFC's external brand and reputation as a leader in market creation and private sector development.
item.dc:creator: World Bank Group
